bio.scholar: Gerald Ford, the thirty-eighth President, was the only person to serve as both Vice President and President without being elected to either office. He assumed the presidency when Nixon resigned amid the Watergate scandal and worked to restore public confidence in government. His controversial decision to pardon Nixon, while deeply unpopular at the time, was later recognized by many as an act of political courage. Ford also managed the final U.S. withdrawal from Vietnam, including the fall of Saigon in April 1975.
bio.explorer: Gerald Ford became the thirty-eighth president in a way nobody expected. He was never elected president OR vice president — he was appointed to both jobs! When President Nixon quit because of the Watergate scandal, Ford stepped in and tried to help the country heal. He pardoned Nixon, which made a lot of people angry, but he thought it was the right thing to do.

text.scholar: Restored public confidence in the presidency following the Watergate scandal and Nixon's resignation, providing stable leadership during a constitutional crisis.
text.explorer: He helped the country trust the presidency again after the Watergate scandal.

sources.citation: Miller Center: gerald ford - Key Events

text.scholar: Managed the final U.S. withdrawal from Vietnam, including the chaotic evacuation of Saigon in April 1975.
text.explorer: He oversaw the end of the Vietnam War, including getting Americans out of Saigon.

sources.citation: Miller Center: gerald ford - Key Events

text.scholar: Pardoned Richard Nixon, a deeply controversial decision later recognized by many as an act of political courage that helped the nation move forward.
text.explorer: He pardoned President Nixon, which was very unpopular but helped the country move on from Watergate.

sources.citation: Miller Center: gerald ford - Key Events

text.scholar: Ford was never elected president or vice president — he became the 38th president through extraordinary constitutional circumstances after both the VP and president left office.
text.explorer: He's the only president who was never elected president OR vice president!

sources.citation: Miller Center: gerald ford - Key Events

text.scholar: He was a star football player at the University of Michigan and received offers to play professionally before choosing law school.
text.explorer: He was a star football player in college and could have gone pro, but he chose to become a lawyer instead.

sources.citation: Miller Center: gerald ford - Key Events

text.scholar: Ford lived to 93 years old, surpassing Ronald Reagan as the longest-lived president at the time of his death in 2006.
text.explorer: He lived to be 93 years old — one of the longest-lived presidents in history.

sources.citation: Miller Center: gerald ford - Key Events
